questions.question: When facing a repetitive task, what solution appeals to you most?
options: Writing a script or software to automate it
Designing a physical tool or device to handle it
Creating a process for people to follow efficiently
Assigning roles so a team can complete it faster

questions.question: Which of these activities excites you the most?
options: Building and deploying a web application
Designing circuits or embedded systems
Optimizing supply chains or workflows
Presenting ideas to stakeholders

questions.question: Which of these activities excites you the most?
options: Designing and testing mechanical or electrical systems
Writing code to control or monitor machines
Planning infrastructure or construction
Managing a project team to deliver on time

questions.question: Which of these activities excites you the most?
options: Researching biological systems or food processes
Designing experiments to test hypotheses
Analyzing data from lab results
Managing research teams or projects

questions.question: When learning something new in your field, you prefer to:
options: Build a working prototype or project immediately
Study theory deeply before attempting anything
Watch others do it first then replicate
Discuss concepts with peers and learn collaboratively

questions.question: How do you feel about spending hours debugging or troubleshooting a complex problem?
options: I enjoy it — finding the root cause is satisfying
I can do it but prefer collaborative problem solving
I prefer someone else handles the deep technical work
I focus more on the big picture than technical details

questions.question: If you had to choose a side project this weekend, which would you pick?
options: Build a personal tool or app to solve a daily problem
Design or fabricate something physical in a workshop
Research and write a report on an emerging technology
Organize a community event or hackathon

questions.question: When you see a complex system, what are you most curious about?
options: How software or algorithms control its behavior
How the physical components interact and function
How the system could be improved or scaled
How teams collaborate to maintain and improve it

questions.question: Which subject or topic did you find most naturally engaging in your studies?
options: Programming, algorithms, or data structures
Physics, circuits, or mechanical systems
Biology, chemistry, or environmental science
Business, economics, or social systems

questions.question: Which area of computer science interests you most?
options: AI, machine learning, and data science
Cybersecurity and network systems
Software development and web technologies
Cloud computing and DevOps

questions.question: Which engineering area interests you most?
options: Electronics, embedded systems, or VLSI
Mechanical design, thermodynamics, or manufacturing
Civil structures, environmental engineering
Chemical processes or material science

questions.question: Which area interests you most in your field?
options: Biotechnology, genetics, or pharmaceutical research
Food science, nutrition, or food processing
Agriculture technology or environmental sustainability
Research data analysis or bioinformatics

questions.question: How do you approach learning a concept outside your comfort zone?
options: I dive deep into textbooks and documentation
I look for practical examples and apply them immediately
I find a mentor or course and follow structured guidance
I avoid it unless absolutely necessary

questions.question: In your ideal career, the work you do every day involves:
options: Solving technical problems using tools specific to your domain
Leading people and making strategic decisions
Research, analysis, and generating new knowledge
Creating products or services customers directly use

questions.question: During a group project, what role do you naturally take?
options: Developing the core technical solution
Planning the strategy and coordinating everyone
Motivating the team and resolving conflicts
Documenting processes and tracking deadlines

questions.question: How do you feel about managing other people's work?
options: I prefer doing the work myself rather than delegating
I enjoy guiding others but like staying hands-on too
I find leading teams more energizing than individual work
I avoid leadership roles — they stress me out

questions.question: When a project faces a major setback, you typically:
options: Immediately analyze what went wrong and fix the technical issue
Communicate with stakeholders and restructure the plan
Motivate the team and keep morale high
Wait for someone else to take charge

questions.question: Your long-term career goal is closer to:
options: Becoming a highly specialized technical expert
Leading a product, company, or large technical team
Running your own business or startup
Contributing to research or academic work

questions.question: When presenting your work to a non-technical audience, you:
options: Find it difficult and prefer technical audiences
Can do it but it requires extra effort
Enjoy translating complex ideas into simple language
Prefer to let others present while you focus on execution

questions.question: When you face a difficult challenge you cannot solve immediately, you usually:
options: Move on quickly to something easier
Try briefly but stop if it feels too hard
Research solutions and keep experimenting
Persist until you eventually figure it out

questions.question: How confident are you in your ability to learn new technical skills on your own?
options: Not confident — I need structured guidance always
Somewhat confident with the right resources
Confident — I can figure things out independently
Very confident — I actively seek self-learning challenges

questions.question: When you receive critical feedback on your work, you typically:
options: Feel discouraged and question your abilities
Accept it but need time to process before moving on
Use it constructively to improve your work
Welcome it eagerly as valuable input for growth

questions.question: How do you feel about taking on a project where you only know 50% of what is required?
options: Anxious — I prefer to be fully prepared before starting
Cautious — I would ask for help or training first
Comfortable — I can learn the rest as I go
Excited — incomplete knowledge makes it more interesting

questions.question: Looking at your current academic or work performance, you feel:
options: Behind where I want to be and unsure how to catch up
Average — doing okay but not standing out
On track — making steady progress toward my goals
Ahead — consistently outperforming my own expectations

questions.question: When making an important decision, your usual approach is:
options: Decide quickly without much analysis
Decide after brief consideration
Analyze options carefully before deciding
Deeply evaluate all possibilities before committing

questions.question: When choosing between two career paths that both seem good, you:
options: Pick the one that pays more without much thought
Ask friends or family and go with their opinion
Research both paths thoroughly and compare them
Reflect deeply on your values and long-term vision before deciding

questions.question: How often do you second-guess major decisions you have already made?
options: Almost always — I frequently doubt my choices
Sometimes — especially when things get difficult
Rarely — I commit and move forward
Never — once I decide, I focus entirely on execution

questions.question: When your career plan does not go as expected, you:
options: Feel lost and unsure what to do next
Wait for external guidance before acting
Reassess and adapt your plan proactively
Immediately identify the next best opportunity and pivot

questions.question: How clear is your sense of what career direction you want to pursue?
options: Very unclear — I have no idea what I want
Vague — I have some ideas but nothing concrete
Fairly clear — I have a direction but not a specific path
Very clear — I know exactly what I want and am working toward it

courses.title: Introduction to Biology — The Secret of Life
Career Planning: Your Career, Your Life
